popularStory.headline: Raimond-Bérenger II de Barcelone
popularStory.description:
Raimond-Bérenger II (1053-1082), aussi connu comme Cheveux d'étoupe pour son épaisse chevelure, fut comte de Barcelone entre 1076 et 1082.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Ramon Berenguer II de Barcelona於1053年11月23日出生在西班牙卡塔倫亞巴薩隆拿巴薩隆拿時，他的父親Ramon Berenguer I Count of Barcelona33歲，母親Almodis de La Marché33歲。他於1078年與Matilda of Apulia在西班牙卡塔倫亞巴薩隆拿艾爾 巴瑟隆斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1082年12月6日在西班牙卡塔倫亞赫羅納桑 費流 德 布薩利享年29歲，埋葬在西班牙卡塔倫亞赫羅納。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ramon Berenguer II de Barcelona于1053年11月23日出生在西班牙卡塔倫亞巴薩隆拿巴薩隆拿时，他的父亲Ramon Berenguer I Count of Barcelona33岁，母亲Almodis de La Marché33岁。他于1078年与Matilda of Apulia在西班牙卡塔倫亞巴薩隆拿艾爾 巴瑟隆斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1082年12月6日在西班牙卡塔倫亞赫羅納桑 費流 德 布薩利享年29岁，埋葬在西班牙卡塔倫亞赫羅納。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Barcelona
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alfredo, Amelita, Elvira, Jaime, Luis, Manuel, Mariano, Orlando, Ramon, Romelia.

Catalan and Spanish: habitational name from Barcelona, the principal city of Catalonia. The placename is of uncertain, certainly pre-Roman, origin. The settlement was established by the Carthaginians, and according to tradition it was named for the Carthaginian ruling house of Barca; the Latin form was Barcino or Barcilo.

variants.name: Barcelo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Carlos, Mario, Francisco, Juan, Ruben, Arturo, Eduardo, Humberto, Ignacio, Jaime.
Catalan (Barceló): apparently from the Latin personal name Barcelonus, a male equivalent of Barcelona, originally denoting someone from the city of Barcelona (see Barcelona ).

nameMeanings.name: Ramon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Berenguer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish and French (southern; Bérenguer): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements ber ‘bear’ + ger ‘spear’, a cognate of French Bérenger (see Berenger ) and Béringer (see Beringer ).

variants.name: Berenger
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Berringer  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Ramon Berenguer II de Barcelona was born on 23 November 1053, in Barcelona, Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain, his father, Ramon Berenguer I Count of Barcelona, was 33 and his mother, Almodis de La Marché, was 33. He married Matilda of Apulia in 1078, in el Barcelonès, Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 6 December 1082, in Sant Feliu de Buixalleu, Girona, Catalonia, Spain, at the age of 29, and was buried in Girona, Catalonia, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ramon Berenguer II de Barcelona est né le 23 novembre 1053, à Barcelona, Barcelona, Catalunya, Espagne, son père, Ramon Berenguer I Count of Barcelona, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Almodis de La Marché, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Matilda of Apulia en 1078, à el Barcelonès, Barcelona, Catalunya, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 6 décembre 1082, à Sant Feliu de Buixalleu, Girona, Catalunya, Espagne, à l’âge de 29 ans, et a été enterré à Girona, Catalunya, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ramon Berenguer II de Barcelona am 23. November 1053 in Barcelona, Barcelona, Catalunya, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ramon Berenguer I Count of Barcelona 33 und seine Mutter Almodis de La Marché 33. Er hat 1078 in el Barcelonès, Barcelona, Catalunya, Spanien Matilda of Apulia geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 6. Dezember 1082 im Alter von 29 Jahren in Sant Feliu de Buixalleu, Girona, Catalunya, Spanien und wurde in Girona, Catalunya, Spanien begraben.

Lui sposato Matilda of Apulia nel 1078, nel el Barcelonès, Barcelona, Catalunya, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. ko.Sant Feliu de Buixalleu,17451,Gérone,Cat: 스페인 카털니아 지로나 산트 펠리우 데 버이스알레우
ko.lifeSummary: Ramon Berenguer II de Barcelona(이)가 1053년 11월 23일 스페인 카털니아 바셀나 바셀나에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ramon Berenguer I Count of Barcelona(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Almodis de La Marché(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1078년 스페인 카털니아 바셀나 엘 바셀e스에서 Matilda of Apulia(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1082년 12월 6일 스페인 카털니아 지로나 산트 펠리우 데 버이스알레우에서 29세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 카털니아 지로나에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ramon Berenguer II de Barcelona родился 23 ноября 1053 г. в местности барселона, барселона, каталуня, Испания, его отцу по имени Ramon Berenguer I Count of Barcelona было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Almodis de La Marché, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda of Apulia в 1078 г. в местности эл барселонeс, барселона, каталуня, Испания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 6 декабря 1082 г. в местности сант фелу де буиксаллеу, гирона, каталуня, Испания в возрасте 29 лет и был похоронен в местности гирона, каталуня, Испания.
